<!-- Mirrored from ~/.claude/commands/steelman.md by scripts/harness/sync-commands-to-prompts.sh -- do not edit directly -->
---
description: Red-team your own last answer — strongest case against it, its blind spots, where a smart dissenter pushes hardest.
---

Before I accept your last answer as given: give me the strongest case against it. What are the blind spots in what you just told me? Where would a smart person who disagrees push back hardest?

Engage genuinely — this is not a token both-sides recap, and it is not an invitation to cave just because you were challenged:

- Argue the opposing position at full strength (steelman, not strawman).
- Name the assumptions your answer depends on that could be wrong.
- Surface what you didn't consider, or conveniently omitted, or got lucky on.

Then state plainly whether your original answer **survives** the challenge, **needs revision** (say exactly what changes), or **was wrong** (say so directly). Go where the reasoning leads — don't reflexively defend, don't reflexively retreat.

$ARGUMENTS
